title: [lora_l2.c:453-456] k_thread_join second timeout uses RX_TIMEOUT_MS directly
description:
Gap found: Thread join timeout sequence not fully analyzed.

Why this was missed: Round 1 covered thread abort but not the timeout values.

The actual bug: In lichen_lora_l2_stop() lines 453-456:
1. First join attempt: K_MSEC(RX_THREAD_QUICK_JOIN_MS) = 100ms
2. Second join attempt: K_MSEC(RX_TIMEOUT_MS) = CONFIG value (default 1000ms)

The LOG_WRN at line 459 says 'timed out after %dms' with value RX_THREAD_QUICK_JOIN_MS + RX_TIMEOUT_MS. This correctly logs total wait time (1100ms default).

The design question: if the thread is blocked in lora_recv() with timeout RX_TIMEOUT_MS, the second join should allow at least that long. The current code does allow this (1000ms second join matches lora_recv timeout). But if CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2_RX_TIMEOUT_MS is increased, the join timeout scales accordingly, which is correct.

Verified correct. Priority 4 - documentation note that join timeout equals RX timeout.
close_reason: Fixed: Added clarifying comment to the second k_thread_join timeout explaining why RX_TIMEOUT_MS is used directly - worst-case scenario where lora_recv() just started when stop flag was set.
